display.purpose: Hero + every promoted section header; animated entrance.

motion.registerRationale: PRODUCT.md Brand Personality: 'display-typography-signature' (Paramount Vista Sans at 112-160px) + 'WE ARE' lockup used as structural language — maps directly to kinetic-display per motion-registers.md selection heuristic.
signatureMoves: Hero 'WE ARE' unmasks letter-by-letter via clip-path: inset(0 0 100% 0) staggered 120ms on load
Section heads wipe in left-to-right via clip-path on word-span children, scroll-driven
Hero IP photography cross-fades on a 4s timed loop (reuses captured slick-carousel behavior)
Large display copy reveals with filter: blur(8px -> 0) on scroll
Brand tiles reveal on scroll with staggered clip-path wipe

iaPriorities.evidence: 12-tile brand grid — load-bearing 'what we are' surface

componentStyle.buttons: Hard-rectangle, electric-blue fill, white label, magenta hover.
componentStyle.cards: Same weighted grid as A; staggered clip-path scroll reveal.

donts: Animate more than display type per viewport

components.name: Kinetic Display Word
components.css: .ds-kinetic{font-family:'Paramount Vista Sans','Arial Narrow',sans-serif;font-weight:700;text-transform:uppercase;font-size:clamp(64px,12vw,200px);line-height:.88;color:#0064FF;margin:0}.ds-kinetic span{display:inline-block;clip-path:inset(0 0 100% 0);animation:dsUnmask .8s cubic-bezier(.16,1,.3,1) forwards}@keyframes dsUnmask{to{clip-path:inset(0 0 0 0)}}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.ds-kinetic span{clip-path:none;animation:none}}

narrative.overview: Variant C keeps Variant A's exact IA and makes one bet: motion is part of the Paramount identity. Paramount Vista Sans becomes the page's structural voice, kinetic — letter-unmask, scroll-wipe, parallax, IP cross-fade — with a full reduced-motion fallback.
keyCharacteristics: Display type promoted into section headers

rules.name: The Color-Is-Constant Rule
rules.body: Motion animates position, scale, mask, and opacity — never hue.

rules.name: The One-Mover Rule
rules.body: Within any viewport, only display type performs the signature motion.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Surfaces never lift; motion supplies the only dynamism.

dos: Do reuse the captured IP cross-fade
donts: Don't animate more than display type per viewport
Don't use motion to hide weak content
